Records.description: Two-loop tardigrade (5-point, non-planar) in $D=4$ with 6 generic masses. Picard-Fuchs operator is order 11 (irreducible) for fully generic masses and kinematics, suggesting K3 surface with Picard number 11. Order 6 for symmetric kinematics.

The non-planar tardigrade graph $(2,2,2)$: 5 vertices, 6 edges, $L=2$, 5 external legs. Two trivalent vertices joined by three chains of length 2. Symanzik polynomials in eq. e:tardigrade. Theorem t:tardigrade: for generic parameters and $D\ge 2$ there is a quartic K3 surface $S$ with six $A_1$ singularities such that $\mathrm{Gr}^W_4 H^4(X_{(2,2,2);D};\mathbb{Q}) = H^2(S;\mathbb{Q})(-1)$ up to mixed Tate factors. This is the smallest two-loop example whose motive is not in $\mathrm{MHS}^{\mathrm{hyp}}$. The Picard-Fuchs operator of the canonical differential form has the same K3 singular locus as the operator computed from the derived quartic $G_{(2,2,2)}(t)$; the two operators differ by the explicit scaling $\sqrt{(p^2 t - (m_1+m_2)^2)(p^2 t - (m_1-m_2)^2)}$. The appendix by Pichon-Pharabod determines the Picard rank of the K3 surface to be 11 and computes its Neron-Severi lattice using certified numerical period integration.
Records.method: graph hypersurface, quadric fibration, blowup, mixed Hodge structure, K3 surface, Picard-Fuchs, Griffiths-Dwork
Records.location: Section sec:tardigrade (Theorem t:tardigrade); Figure fig:tardigrade; eq. e:tardigrade; Appendix by Pichon-Pharabod
